data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: WhatsApp employs a scalable microservices architecture to manage user interactions and features efficiently.
data.technical_spec: The system is composed of multiple microservices, each responsible for specific functionalities like messaging, media sharing, and user authentication, typically using RESTful APIs.

data.title: End-to-End Encryption
data.description: WhatsApp uses end-to-end encryption to ensure that only the communicating users can read the messages, enhancing privacy.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes the Signal Protocol to encrypt messages, which means even WhatsApp cannot access the message contents.

data.title: Cross-Platform Synchronization
data.description: Provides real-time message and data synchronization across multiple platforms, including iOS, Android, and web.
data.technical_spec: Relies on cloud services and persistent connections to ensure data is consistent and up-to-date across user devices.

data.title: Messaging Protocol
data.description: A custom messaging protocol that ensures reliable and fast message delivery across global networks.
data.technical_spec: Based on the XMPP protocol but heavily modified for optimization and reliability at scale.

data.title: Data Storage and Management
data.description: WhatsApp efficiently handles large volumes of data with a robust storage management system.
data.technical_spec: Uses a mix of on-premise and cloud solutions, including database technologies like MySQL or NoSQL databases for scalable storage.
